finding_results.notes: Noreen now names the concrete downstream harm of Della's withholding in rent, delay, and filing costs; Lucille keeps the scene at procedural fact and unresolved consequence; the truck and closing beats keep the Bledsoe household's material loss live without certifying Della's line as morally right.
finding_results.summary: The aftermath now stays inside unresolved cost instead of resolving into endorsement.

finding_results.notes: The hearing no longer claims Della brought or copied a fever-ground extract before the theft. It now grounds Gabriel's identification in Silas Wynn's filed statement, exhumation notes, bodily markers, the pocketwatch, the parcel map, and Della's testimony, and the ruling relies on that same established evidence set.
finding_results.summary: The invented pre-theft fever-ground document was removed and replaced with previously established on-page proof.

finding_results.notes: Della's testimony now places Gabriel's grave on the south edge of the lower fever ground, below the lightning-scarred cedar root and east of the child fever row, removing the north-side contradiction.
finding_results.summary: Gabriel's burial geometry now matches the established cemetery placement.

summary: Removed the invented fever-ground documentary support, corrected Gabriel Wolfe's grave placement, and rewrote the aftermath so the chapter ends in ongoing material and ethical cost rather than moral reassurance.
